mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in >60% of late-cycle expansion periods, or the 5-day return distribution shows no significant mean difference.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 2.5 percent annual threshold triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >70% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of GDP growth periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding one percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive returns in >65% of triggered months, or the decline magnitude shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent equity performance.
